title: Version Control Basics
source_outline: What Git is and why it matters: why professionals never work without version control
The basic loop: repository, commit, push
GitHub Classroom: how the cohort repo works; accepting an assignment
Working via the web interface: editing files in the browser; reading commit history
Good habits: README as the front door; small, descriptive commits; branching basics (main vs feature) - light intro; .gitignore for API keys - intro

depth_signals: Implementation section warranted (numbered end-to-end browser workflow)
topic spans multiple technologies (Git, GitHub, GitHub Classroom)

next_assessment.title: Python Foundations Diagnostic
